steps.title: Use the intercept shortcut
steps.content: A $y$-intercept is where a graph crosses the $y$-axis. Every point on the $y$-axis has $x=0$, so this is a graph-reading question: look only at the point where the curve meets the vertical axis.

steps.title: Read the point from the graph
steps.content: The curve crosses the $y$-axis at the marked point $(0,3)$. The question asks for the $y$-coordinate, not the whole point, so the value is $3$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Because the $y$-intercept is $(0,3)$, the $y$-coordinate of that intercept is 3.
